puzzle.title: The Voice from the Burning Bush
puzzle.intro: Unravel the identity of a profound figure whose journey began with a miraculous encounter and whose destiny was intertwined with the liberation of a downtrodden people. This individual was chosen for a monumental task, facing immense challenges with divine support.
clues: I was born into a time of great oppression, where infants of my people were being killed.
My early life involved being placed in a river by my parent, yet I was found and raised in the very household of my people's oppressor.
I once fled a city after unintentionally causing the death of an individual during a dispute.
I received a divine message and a miraculous staff at a sacred place, where I was commanded to confront a powerful ruler.
My brother was appointed as a helper to me in my mission, due to a perceived impediment in my speech.
I led my people across a parted sea, escaping the pursuit of a tyrannical army.

trial.label: Trial/Event
options: Being thrown into a fiery furnace
The destruction of the people of Thamud

outcome.label: Outcome
options: The city of Sodom was overturned
The people were swallowed by the earth
The great flood engulfed the land
Pharaoh and his army were drowned
The city walls collapsed from a cry
outcome.answer: Pharaoh and his army were drowned

puzzle.arabic: وَلَقَدْ أَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَىٰ مُوسَىٰٓ أَنْ أَسْرِ بِعِبَادِى فَٱضْرِبْ لَهُمْ طَرِيقًۭا فِى ٱلْبَحْرِ يَبَسًۭا لَّا تَخَـٰفُ دَرَكًۭا وَلَا تَخْشَىٰ
puzzle.verse: And We had inspired to Moses, "Travel by night with My servants and strike for them a dry path through the sea; you will not fear being overtaken [by Pharaoh] nor be afraid [of drowning]." (20:77)
